heading: Approval of employment and compensation of committee employees by House standing committees
label: 4311 Approval of employment and compensation of committee employees by House standing committees
text: Standing committees of the House shall have authority to approve the employment and compensation of committee employees (other than special and select committee employees) from the effective date of the beginning of each Congress, or such subsequent date as their service commenced.
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 72a–1b of this titleSection is based on House Resolution No. 16, Eighty-seventh Congress, , which was enacted into permanent law by .Jan. 3, 1961Pub. L. 87–130

Increases in CompensationIncreases in compensation for House officers and employees under authority of Federal Salary Act of 1967 (), Federal Pay Comparability Act of 1970 (), and Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1988 (), see sections 4531 and 4532 of this title, and Salary Directives of Speaker of the House, set out as notes under those sections.Pub. L. 90–206Pub. L. 91–656Pub. L. 100–202
